subject: The $100 Receipt Trap
previewText: That store slip is worth more than you think.
intro_text:
Hi {{ contact.firstname|default("there") }},

You know that receipt sitting in your truck console right now? The one from the feed store, or the parts run, or the chemical purchase you made last week?

It's not just paper. It's audit protection. It's proof of what your inputs actually cost. And when it disappears, crumpled in a pocket, blown out on the barn floor, never printed from an email — you lose more than a piece of paper. You lose the ability to prove what you spent, what you own, and whether you actually made money this year.

Here's where it hits hardest:

  • After a disaster, that receipt is the difference between getting paid what your inventory was worth and getting whatever the adjuster decides.
  • At year-end, it's how you know whether your per-head cost or your cost per acre is actually working or whether you're just trading dollars and hoping for the best.
  • With a vendor dispute, it's your leverage. A bad batch of seed or a missing rebate is a phone call when you have proof. It's a write-off when you don't.
secondary_text: Ambrook makes this a 10-second habit instead of a year-end scramble. Snap a photo from your phone, and the app reads the line items, matches it to the transaction, and files it, no desk time, no shoebox, no spreadsheet.
